response: Originating from 1960s U.S. defense research and the 1969 ARPANET demonstration, the internet evolved through critical technical milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. After reaching mainstream users via 1990s browsers and surviving the 2001 dot-com crash, it rapidly scaled through social media, 2007 smartphones, and cloud computing. Ultimately, the text argues that the internet has transformed from a niche academic and military network into an indispensable global infrastructure. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly every facet of modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
